observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO-Designate and Municipal Engineer, to conduct physical inventory, prepare the RPCPPE, and submit a copy of the reports to the Audit Team; and
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets which are necessary to support the accuracy, existence and reliability of the balance of the PPE account in the financial statements; and
3. Instruct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO-Designate, to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, completeness and condition.
observations.managementAction: Management had created its Inventory Committee responsible for the preparation and submission of the RPCPPE. As of this writing, the concerned officials are in the process of identifying the different PPEs owned by the LGU for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs and reconcile the same regularly with the Stock Cards of the GSO-Designate to avoid possible theft and misappropriation; and
2. Department heads/requisitioning units to prepare the RIS for every request of supplies issued for submission to the GSO; and
3. GSO-designate to maintain stock cards to account for the receipt and disposition of supplies and materials; and submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken by Management. The Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate are in the process of preparing the SLCs and Stock Cards for each item of inventory. Ongoing preparation of the forms needed and dissemination of the procedures to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that Management:
1. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of stale checks and comply with the procedures in the handling of stale checks as stated in Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA.
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank – SAGANA Fund and Cash in Bank – SEF in the books of accounts.
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to take-up all valid reconciling items to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of stale checks and comply with the procedures in the handling of stale checks as stated in Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank – SAGANA Fund and Cash in Bank – SEF in the books of accounts. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to take-up all valid reconciling items to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to implement measures to ensure prompt and correct reporting and remittances of collections in order to reflect the correct cash balances and income in the financial statements and prevent misappropriation or misuse of funds as follows:
a. To require collecting officers to prepare duly signed RCDs at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily and intact in order to ensure correctness of recorded cash balances and income in the Treasury reports and in the financial statements; and
b. To require all AOs to exercise due diligence in the handling of government funds, particularly on the collection, remittance and deposit of collections, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against erring AOs.

We, likewise, recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize income in the year it was earned in compliance with the generally accepted accounting principles to provide accurate and reliable information to the users of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented. Require collecting officers to prepare duly signed RCDs at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily and intact. Require all AOs to exercise due diligence in the handling of government funds, particularly on the collection, remittance and deposit of collections, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against erring AOs. The Municipal Accountant to recognize income in the year it was earned in compliance with generally accepted accounting principles.

It must also be assured that the 20% Development Fund and Calamity Fund shall be utilized for the purpose for which the funds were intended. Also, the Municipal Treasurer every time the Internal Revenue Allotment is received, set aside the funds intended for the purpose and must see to it that the banks’ balance reconciles with the book balance for the funds.

We further recommend that Management continuously take necessary measures to lessen the cash deficit of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management adhere with the provisions prescribed in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those of the third parties involved in the execution of the government projects.
